entry.name: alteryx-aidin

This App-ID covers Alteryx AiDIN, which augments the Alteryx platform with generative AI capabilities. Currently, only the "Magic Documents" feature is covered.

entry.name: amazon-bedrock
entry.description: Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that offers a choice of high-performing foundation models (FMs) from leading AI companies like AI21 Labs, Anthropic, Cohere, Meta, Mistral AI, Stability AI, and Amazon via a single API, along with a broad set of capabilities user need to build generative AI applications with security, privacy, and responsible AI. This App-ID covers the traffic of Amazon Bedrock.

entry.name: amazon-codewhisperer

entry.name: amazon-sagemaker

entry.name: amazon-sagemaker-groundtruth

entry.name: amazon-transcribe

entry.name: simatic-logon-encrypted-login
entry.description: SIMATIC Logon is a tool for system-wide user administration and provides central access protection for SIMATIC applications and plant areas. With SIMATIC Logon, users are centrally managed outside the application or HMI device and the user logs on to and off from the application through the SIMATIC Logon Service. This App-ID covers SIMATIC Logon Service traffic that is sent using TLS encryption.

entry.name: simatic-logon-unencrypted-login
entry.description: SIMATIC Logon is a tool for system-wide user administration and provides central access protection for SIMATIC applications and plant areas. With SIMATIC Logon, users are centrally managed outside the application or HMI device and the user logs on to and off from the application through the SIMATIC Logon Service. This App-ID covers SIMATIC Logon Service traffic that is sent unencrypted.

entry.name: br-ansl
entry.description: B&R Industrial Automation GmbH is a manufacturer of automation technology and is a leading global solution provider for automating machines and factories. B&R ANSL (Automation Network Service Link) is a communication protocol used to communicate with a B&R PLC and an employee workstation running PLC configuration software such as B&R Automation Studio. This App-ID covers communication from a B&R PLC to a workstation running PLC configuration and monitoring software such as B&R Automation Studio.

entry.name: br-sdm
entry.description: B&R Industrial Automation GmbH is a manufacturer of automation technology and is a leading global solution provider for automating machines and factories. The B&R System Diagnostics Manager (SDM), allows management and monitoring of factory automation equipment using a traditional web-browser. This App-ID cover communication from the B&R SDM web interface to a B&R automation PLC. This App-ID covers communication between a B&R PLC and a device running the B&R System Diagnostics Manager (SDM) software.

entry.name: fanuc-focas-axis-names

entry.name: panasonic-mewtocol-com-exrt

This App-ID covers Panasonic MEWTOCOL-COM (Computer Link) extended read status (EXRT) command.

entry.name: emerson-bsap
entry.description: Bristol Standard Asynchronous/Synchronous Protocol (BSAP) is an industrial automation protocol developed by Bristol Babcock and managed by Emerson. BSAP is a poll oriented communication protocol for horizontal, vertical, and multi-layer networks. BSAP is equally well suited to both synchronous high-speed local networks and asynchronous low-speed wide area networks. This App-ID covers BSAP communication over UDP.

entry.name: redlion-crimson
entry.description: Crimson by Red Lion is a powerful next-generation programming software for Red Lion's Human Machine Interfaces (HMIs), modular controllers, temperature controllers, and panel meters. Crimson supports over 300 protocol drivers, including Modbus RTU and TCP/IP, EtherNet/IP, Emerson ROC, and both DNP3 serial and Ethernet communications. With an intuitive, drag-and-drop graphical user interface and support for multiple simultaneous conversions, customers can easily integrate virtually any PLC, RTU, or industrial asset to the most popular IIoT clouds or SCADA platforms. This App-ID covers communication from a workstation running Crimson software to a Red Lion PLC.

entry.name: ctc-binary-protocol
entry.description: The Control Technology Corporation (CTC) Binary Protocol is a protocol developed by Control Technology Corporation. Control Technology Corporation specializes in providing automation solutions including programmable logic controllers (PLCs), motion controllers, and related industrial automation equipment. The CTC Binary Protocol is a high-speed, non-routable protocol that has checksum and error reporting capabilities. This App-ID covers ctc-binary-protocol from a program running on a host computer to a CTC PLC.

entry.name: gquic
entry.description: gQUIC (Google Quick UDP Internet Connections) is a protocol made by Google and originally designed for transmission of HTTP/2 frames. It works with Chrome and gives the incentive to standardize QUIC. This App-ID covers the traffic of gQUIC.

entry.name: quic-base
entry.description: QUIC (Quick UDP Internet Connections, pronounced quick) is an experimental transport layer network protocol developed by Google. QUIC supports a set of multiplexed connections between two endpoints over User Datagram Protocol (UDP), and was designed to provide security protection equivalent to TLS/SSL, along with reduced connection and transport latency, and bandwidth estimation in each direction to avoid congestion. QUIC's main goal is to optimize connection-oriented web applications currently using TCP.

This App-ID covers SICK SOPAS CoLa A traffic.

entry.name: target-communication-framework
entry.description: Target Communication Framework (TCF) is a vendor-neutral, lightweight, extensible network protocol for communicating with embedded systems (targets). It can be used to unify many currently independent communication links, thus saving resources and making setup and configuration easier than in current embedded development scenarios. This App-ID covers the TCF protocol.
